tool_args.label: Bronx Bus Network Redesign Final Plan Addendum

payload.title: The Bronx Bus Network Redesign Final Plan Addendum

payload.entity_name: New York City Department of Transportation

payload.description: Multi-year planning effort to improve Local, Limited and Select Bus Service routes in the Bronx. Paused due to COVID-19 pandemic, restarted August 2021, planned for implementation Summer 2022.

payload.corridor_name: Pelham Parkway, Fordham Road, and West 207 Street
payload.street: Pelham Parkway / Fordham Road / West 207 Street

payload.description: Bx28 retains original routing; proposed streamlining avoided. Frequency improvements still planned.
Bx34 retains original routing on Valentine Avenue and Bainbridge Avenue; proposed Webster Avenue corridor alignment dropped. Frequencies remain the same as today.
Bx6 SBS implementation delayed until 2023 to coincide with MetroCard retirement and OMNY full deployment. Off-board fare machines not installed just for one year.
Bx5 schedule changes postponed until 2023 when Bx6 SBS realignment is implemented.
Bx6 Local schedule changes postponed until 2023 when Bx6 SBS realignment is implemented.

payload.table_title: Route Alignment Changes, Schedule Improvements, and Bus Stop Changes by Route
payload.notes: Bx6 to be implemented in 2023 with Bx6 SBS alignment and Bx5 schedule changes. Q50 LTD schedule improvement during off-peak periods only.

payload.description: MTA held a public hearing on the Bronx Bus Network Redesign proposed Final Plan.
MTA paused the bus network redesign initiative due to the COVID-19 pandemic. Project remained paused for approximately 18 months.
Acting MTA Chair and CEO Janno Lieber and Interim NYCT President Craig Cipriano joined NYC DOT Commissioner Henry Gutman to announce restart of Bronx Bus Network Redesign.
Planned implementation of Bronx Bus Network Redesign.
Bx6 SBS implementation delayed until 2023 to coincide with MetroCard retirement and OMNY full deployment.
NYC DOT completed new bus lanes on East 149th Street.
NYC DOT completed center running bus lanes on E.L. Grant Highway.
NYC DOT completed a Busway on West 181st Street.

payload.claim_text: This plan proposes 14 route alignment changes and two new routes.
payload.subject: redesign_plan

payload.claim_text: Four routes will be extended and three will make key connections to subway stations.
payload.subject: redesign_plan

payload.claim_text: This plan improves frequency on nine routes along 11 corridors.
payload.subject: redesign_plan

payload.context: Proposed removal of bus stops across the Bronx network.
Each bus stop removed from a route saves an average of 20 seconds.

payload.raw_value_text: averaging less than two extra minutes to reach
payload.context: Added travel time to the next closest bus stop after removal.

payload.claim_text: All-door boarding will be launched on all bus routes in 2023.
payload.subject: fare_policy

payload.description: Bronx Bus Network Redesign proposed Final Plan released.

payload.raw_value_text: 46 corridors in the Bronx and Manhattan
payload.context: Corridors analyzed for bus priority treatments.
Corridors selected for bus priority treatments in the Bronx.

payload.description: All-door boarding to be launched on all bus routes, coinciding with MetroCard retirement and OMNY full deployment.
Improvements include new bus lanes, transit signal priority (TSP), bus boarders, and curb management.

payload.claim_text: There are currently no changes to express routes at this time.
payload.subject: redesign_plan

payload.claim_text: NYC DOT analyzed 46 corridors in the Bronx and Manhattan and selected 10 of the highest-ranking corridors to implement a variety of bus priority treatments.
payload.subject: nycdot_bus_priority_analysis

payload.claim_text: A map of the Bronx shows bus priority projects with labels for 181 St/Washington Br, University Av, Fordham Rd, Tremont Av, E.L. Grant Hwy, 149 St, Gun Hill Rd, Story Av, and Pelham Bay Park Station Area. Legend indicates Completed (blue), In Implementation (purple), and Forthcoming (orange).
payload.subject: map_caption

payload.claim_text: Routes with Alignment Changes: Bx4, Bx4A, Bx6, Bx11, Bx13, Bx15, Bx18, Bx24, Bx28, Bx38, Bx29, Bx30, Bx35, Bx36, Bx40, Bx42, M100, M125. Routes with Schedule Improvements: Bx1, Bx2, Bx3, Bx4, Bx4A, Bx5, Bx7, Bx8, Bx9, Bx10, Bx11, Bx12, Bx13, Bx15, Bx16, Bx17, Bx18, Bx19, Bx20, Bx21, Bx22, Bx23, Bx24, Bx25, Bx26, Bx27, Bx28, Bx38, Bx29, Bx30, Bx31, Bx32, Bx33, Bx34, Bx35, Bx36, Bx39, Bx40, Bx42, Bx41, Bx41 SBS, Bx46, Q50 LTD (off-peak), M100, M125. Routes with Bus Stop Changes: Bx4A, Bx11, Bx18, Bx24, Bx28, Bx29, Bx30, Bx35, Bx36, Bx40, Bx42, M100, M125.
payload.subject: route_changes_table

payload.claim_text: Simplify the Network: More direct routes bring bus customers to key destinations faster. This plan proposes 14 route alignment changes and two new routes.
payload.subject: redesign_strategy_simplify

payload.claim_text: Enhance Connectivity: Four routes will be extended and three will make key connections to subway stations.
payload.subject: redesign_strategy_connectivity

payload.claim_text: Improve Frequencies: Prioritizing an all-day frequent network. This plan improves frequency on nine routes along 11 corridors.
payload.subject: redesign_strategy_frequency

payload.claim_text: Balance Bus Stops: Each bus stop removed saves an average of 20 seconds. This plan proposes removing about 400 bus stops. Careful consideration given to ridership, distance between stops, transfers, community facilities, and local demographics.
payload.subject: redesign_strategy_stops

payload.claim_text: Expand Bus Priority: NYC DOT has done extensive work to give buses preferential treatment on streets where appropriate to improve bus speeds and reliability.
payload.subject: redesign_strategy_priority
